channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: SBS: «No quisimos poner un camisón de fuerza», por qué el regulador peruano evitó una regla única para comunicar incidentes
item.description: Una nueva norma peruana opta por no establecer una receta única para comunicar incidentes de seguridad, bajo el criterio de que cada entidad financiera debe adaptar estos protocolos a su realidad operativa, en lugar de seguir un esquema uniforme para todo el sistema. Accede al análisis que impulsa la toma de decisiones en la banca latinoamericana Este contenido exclusivo está disponible solo para miembros registrados de iupana. Regístrate gratis o inicia sesión para leer el artículo completo y descubrir: Perspectivas estratégicas sobre innovación, regulación y tendencias en banca digital y fintech Entrevistas con líderes que están transformando el sector financiero en la región Reportes y estudios que anticipan los cambios clave del mercado Acceso prioritario a información para tomar decisiones informadas y fortalecer tu liderazgo Únete a la comunidad de ejecutivos que marcan la diferencia en la transformación digital de la banca.

item.title: AM Best confirma los ratings de las principales entidades operativas de GCO
item.description: AM Best ha ratificado en ‘A’ (Excelente) la nota de fortaleza financiera de Occident y en ‘a+’ (Excelente) la calificación crediticia de emisor a largo plazo, con perspectiva estable en ambos casos.

item.title: Unión Alcoyana amplía su seguro de decesos con nuevas coberturas
item.description: Unión Alcoyana Seguros ha actualizado su seguro de decesos con la incorporación de nuevas garantías opcionales, entre las que destacan una cobertura de ayuda a la dependencia y una indemnización por el fallecimiento del perro, con el objetivo de ampliar la protección más allá del servicio funerario tradicional.

item.title: Los nichos que anticipan el futuro del seguro
item.description: Durante años, el desarrollo del negocio asegurador se ha apoyado en una lógica bien conocida: relaciones estables, pólizas recurrentes y clientes vinculados a largo plazo. Sin embargo, en paralelo —casi de forma silenciosa— han ido emergiendo soluciones que responden a un patrón distinto de comportamiento. Son los llamados seguros especializados o seguros de nicho.

item.title: Executives on the Move at SiriusPoint, Canopius Group, Badger Mutual
item.description: Canopius Group appointed Nadine Moore as chief executive officer of its U.S. She will report to Group CEO Neil Robertson and will join the group leadership team to deliver on Canopius’ long-term strategy in the region. Badger Mutual Insurance Company has named Shane Paltzer as director of strategic growth.

item.title: Beyond the Weather News Bulletins: How Insurers Can Improve NatCat Responses
item.description: When a wildfire, hurricane, tornado or flood is unfolding, the news media plays a critical role in alerting local populations. News media coverage that is built to inform the public doesn’t translate into actionable information a claims team can use to understand which of its insured locations sits inside the footprint. But in the decade my colleagues and I have spent working through these events alongside carriers, I have seen how little of that coverage actually helps the claims executive who has to respond.

item.title: Aon appoints Joe Liang as executive chairman of reinsurance for greater China
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Marsh Risk bolsters Asia marine team with trio appointment
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Prosper Capital LLP enters creditors’ voluntary liquidation
item.description: On 1 June 2026, Prosper Capital LLP (Prosper) went into creditors’ voluntary liquidation. Its designated members took steps to place Prosper into creditors’ voluntary liquidation. Prosper was also responsible for the activities of its appointed representative, Crowd2Let Capital Limited (Crowd2Let) (FRN: 743371).

item.title: Mexico Extends Its Venture Lead Over Brazil As More Global VCs Enter Latin America
item.description: For the third quarter in the past year, Mexico-based companies raised more venture capital in Q2 than their Brazilian counterparts, Crunchbase data on startup funding in Latin America shows, as Silicon Valley investors including Founders Fund and Andreessen Horowitz (a16z) led some of the largest deals in the region. (We expect the Q2 deal count to rise somewhat over time, however, as seed rounds in particular are commonly reported weeks or months after they close.) There were five nine-figure raises in Latin America in the second quarter, and as mentioned earlier, three of those were by Mexico City-based companies. Startups in Latin America raised a combined $1.36 billion across seed- and growth-stage deals in the second quarter, up 47% year over year and 22% from the first quarter.

item.title: Near-miss for Mexico’s parametric catastrophe bond on Friday’s M7.3 earthquake
item.description: After a magnitude 7.3 earthquake struck just off the southern coast of Mexico on Friday, analysis suggests this event was a near-miss for attaching the parametric trigger of one tranche of notes from the country’s World Bank facilitated IBRD CAR Mexico 2024 catastrophe bond transaction. Mexico has a robust natural disaster risk transfer and insurance program in place, at the heart of which sits its World Bank supported catastrophe bond issuances, as well as its traditional parametric insurance program. The Government of Mexico had secured $420 million of parametric disaster insurance protection against earthquakes and Atlantic hurricanes through the World Bank facilitated IBRD CAR Mexico 2024 catastrophe bond transaction, issued in early April 2024.

item.title: Bermuda cat bond and ILS registrations bolstered by first-time sponsors in H1 2026
item.description: Bermuda again saw strong registration numbers of new catastrophe bond, insurance-linked security (ILS) and alternative or third-party reinsurance capital related entities in the first-half of 2026 and while the total dropped slightly compared to the prior year, first-time cat bond market entrants bolstered the total. Notably, of the 10 SPI’s registered in Bermuda in the first-half of this year to be used for catastrophe bond issuances, 7 of them were for first-time sponsors to enter the market with their debut cat bond deals. Hence, the number of new registrations has been relatively steady over-time in total, but the number of SPI’s established for new sponsors has been rising over-time, which is both a sign of the health of the catastrophe bond market, the broadening acceptance of cat bonds as part of sponsors reinsurance towers and also the continued dominance of Bermuda in providing a home for cat bond issuance vehicles.

item.title: Price Forbes names Adam Chapman as Executive Director, Construction and Energy
item.description: Price Forbes, the independent specialist insurance and reinsurance broker, has confirmed the appointment of Adam Chapman as Executive Director, Construction and Energy. Chapman will join Price Forbes in June 2027, reporting to Paul Lewis, Chief Executive Officer of Construction at Price Forbes. In the role, Chapman will be responsible for leading the placement and delivery of insurance solutions across oil, gas, petrochemical and major construction projects.

item.title: LM Re appoints Tom Spurgeon as Global Head of Cyber Reinsurance
item.description: Liberty Mutual Reinsurance Group (LM Re), part of Liberty Mutual Insurance Group, has announced the appointment of Tom Spurgeon as Global Head of Cyber Reinsurance. Barber said, “We are delighted to announce Tom as our Global Head of Cyber Reinsurance at LM Re which is testament to the investment in talent at Liberty. Based in London, Spurgeon will report to Susan Barber, Head of Financial Risks, Reinsurance at LM Re.

item.title: AM Best affirms Vantage’s credit ratings post recent leadership changes
item.description: AM Best has affirmed that the credit ratings for Vantage Risk Ltd. According to AM Best, “The appointments offer additional experienced industry leadership to the Vantage Group.” The leadership changes follow Howard Hughes Holdings’ acquisition of Vantage Group in an all-cash transaction valued at approximately $2.1 billion, after which AM Best affirmed Vantage Risk’s ‘A-’ rating last week. AM Best said it will continue to monitor the leadership transition through the remainder of Hendrick’s tenure and Gansberg’s move into the CEO role, and will take rating action if any developments affect Vantage’s creditworthiness.

item.title: Update: Iran and US Widen Attacks, Raising Fears for Gulf Shipping and Water Supplies
item.description: Read more: Iran Warns Strait of Hormuz Is a ‘Red Line’ and Will Resist Until the End The conflict has also escalated with attacks on desalination facilities, raising the prospect of water shortages across parts of the Gulf and opening a new front in a war that is increasingly threatening civilian infrastructure. Oil prices briefly jumped above $90 a barrel after renewed disruption to shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, but later pared gains after Iran’s Foreign Ministry said mediators had recently presented proposals to Tehran, signaling that diplomatic contacts remain active. President Donald Trump, under mounting political pressure at home over rising gasoline prices, defended the latest strikes on Iran as retaliation for the deaths of as many as three American service members in recent Iranian attacks.

item.title: Dynacom Vessel Catches Fire Near Oman After Projectile Hit
item.description: A vessel operated by Greek shipping firm Dynacom caught fire after it was struck by an unknown projectile 8 nautical miles northwest of Oman’s Kumzar overlooking the Strait of Hormuz, maritime security officials said on Monday. The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations agency said the crew safely abandoned the vessel, now adrift and recovered by a tug boat, following its report that the vessel was on fire. “Vanguard is aware that the Malta-flagged tanker Kavomaleas caught fire while transiting the Strait of Hormuz … at approximately 1924 UTC,” British maritime risk management group Vanguard said.

item.title: Viewpoint: Do Heart Attack Patients Have More Micro and Nanoplastics in Their Blood?
item.description: Researchers found that people who smoke were six times more likely to have detectable micro- and nanoplastics in the blood supplying their hearts than non-smokers. Micro- and nanoplastics were detected in 84% of patients who had suffered a heart attack compared with 40% of those with chronic coronary disease and 32% of those with normal coronary arteries. Even more notable, every smoker who was also exposed to higher levels of air pollution had plastics detected in their blood, compared with just 12.5% of people who neither smoked nor experienced high pollution exposure.

item.title: Aon expands Data Center Lifecycle Insurance Program to $5 billion
item.description: Aon is expanding the capacity for its Data Center Lifecycle Insurance Program to $5 billion while broadening the integrated risk solutions that support digital infrastructure assets from development through long-term operations. As clients build larger and more complex data center portfolios, they need access to greater insurance capacity alongside solutions that strengthen resilience throughout the asset lifecycle. First introduced in 2025, DCLP is a multi-line insurance solution designed to support data center projects from construction through ongoing operations.

item.title: Chime launches Chime Invest
item.description: Banking startup Chime announced the launch of Chime Invest, which allows users to buy stocks and ETFs commission-free. The new offering also gives users the option to choose an “expert-built,” diversified portfolio personalized to their goals and risk profile, managed by Atomic Invest, an SEC registered investment adviser. Managed portfolios have no account balance minimums and have no management fees for Chime Prime members, a 0.10% annual management fee for Chime Plus members, and 0.25% for all other Chime members.

item.title: California Utility Says It’s Offered $750M in Program for Eaton Fire Victims
item.description: Southern California Edison announced that more than $750 million has been offered through its Wildfire Recovery Compensation Program to compensate people impacted by one of the massive 2025 Los Angeles wildfires. The company said there’s continued interest in its voluntary program for eligible residents impacted by the Eaton Fire. Related: California Utility Bills Are 20% Higher Due to Wildfires Offers are being made in an average of roughly 35 days, according to SCE.

item.title: FAA Letting Boeing Resume Clearing 737, 787 Jets to Fly
item.description: In 2025, the FAA allowed Boeing to resume issuing airworthiness certificates for some 737 MAX and 787 airplanes, with the planemaker and the regulator taking on the role of issuing sign-offs during alternating weeks. regained the authority to issue airworthiness certificates for its 737 Max and 787 Dreamliner jets prior to delivery, powers that U.S. “It reflects the FAA’s confidence in Boeing’s ability to issue airworthiness certificates under FAA oversight,” it added.

item.title: US Considers Creating Finra-Like Watchdog to Vet Top AI Models
item.description: The Trump administration is considering plans for an independent regulator to vet the safety of artificial intelligence models with industry input, after Silicon Valley leaders complained about the ad-hoc nature of recent U.S. Such an approach would offer more certainty for leading AI labs like Anthropic PBC, which last month was hit with U.S. export controls that led it to temporarily disable its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models, and OpenAI, which made significant changes at the government’s request before releasing its latest Sol model.

item.title: Insurance lessons from one of China’s deadliest industrial accidents in recent years
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Marsh expands US$2.7bn data centre construction insurance facility to Asia
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Wang Fuk Court insurance rights to shift to administrator under government buyout: reports
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Best of Artemis, week ending July 19th 2026
item.description: Ten most read articles on Artemis.bm, week ending July 19th 2026: This is not every article published on Artemis during the last week, just the most popular among our readers over the last seven days. Here are the ten most popular news articles, week ending July 19th 2026, covering catastrophe bonds, ILS, reinsurance capital and related risk transfer topics. To ensure you never miss a thing subscribe to the weekly Artemis email newsletter updates or get our email alerts for every article we publish.

item.title: Ledger Investing’s Korra appoints Yisheng Bu as Chief Actuary
item.description: Korra, the Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) platform from Ledger Investing that is focused on casualty ILS, has announced that it has appointed Yisheng Bu as Chief Actuary, who joins the firm after 13 years of service at Sompo International. Korra was launched last year by Ledger Investing, an insurance technology (insurtech) and casualty ILS specialist. Bu, who is based in New York, will provide actuarial support for transactions in the property and casualty ILS and collateralized reinsurance markets, helping drive the next phase of Korra’s growth and product innovation.

item.title: Aon appoints Johannes Glasl as Chief Regions & Client Officer
item.description: Aon, a global professional services firm, has appointed Johannes Glasl in a newly created role of Chief Regions & Client Officer, effective July 1st, 2026. In his new role, Glasl will further drive the development of the broker’s regional positioning – especially in the North and East regions. Glasl as a member of the Executive Leadership Team in Germany will help shape the business’ strategic development.

item.title: ACORD Solutions Group adds new members to its Board of Directors
item.description: ACORD, the global standards-setting organisation for the insurance sector, has appointed four new members: Kasey Connors, Nidhi Howell, Christina Lucas, and Spero Zacharias to its Board of Directors. His decades of volunteer service in the ACORD community have included terms as both Chair (2017 to 2019) and Vice-Chair of the ACORD Board of Directors. Zacharias, Senior Vice President, Global Information Technology Major Initiatives at Chubb, will chair the Board.

item.title: Allianz Jio Re hits ₹266 crore gross premiums in first full quarter of operations
item.description: Allianz Jio Reinsurance, the 50:50 joint venture between Allianz Group and Jio Financial Services Limited (JFSL), underwrote gross premiums of ₹266 crore in its first full quarter of operations, securing lead reinsurer status on major treaty programs across prominent Indian private insurers. Alongside gross premiums of ₹266 crore in its first full quarter of operations, Allianz Jio Re reportedly established robust retrocession frameworks to maximise capital efficiency and advanced exposure management. Following final regulatory approval in March 2026, Allianz Jio Re officially commenced underwriting operations in India .

item.title: Texas Big Rigs Parts Distributor Faces $264K Fine After Worker Fatality
item.description: Department of Labor has cited a Texas big rig parts distributer for 16 serious safety violations after an investigation into a worker fatality found the company exposed workers to confined space and other safety hazards. 7, 2026, after an employee asphyxiated while inspecting a tanker trailer. The department’s Occupational Safety and Health Administration initiated an inspection into FleetPride on Jan.

item.title: People Moves: Cottingham & Butler Expands Property & Casualty Leadership
item.description: Cottingham & Butler , a privately held insurance broker based in Dubuque, Iowa, announced a series of leadership appointments across its Property & Casualty business, reinforcing the firm’s specialized expertise and its commitment to industry-leading insurance brokerage services. Cottingham & Butler offers a full suite of capabilities including property & casualty, employee benefits, captive and alternative risk programs, claims and benefits administration, wellness and medical management, and safety and loss control. Topics Leadership Property Property Casualty Casualty

item.title: Ohio-Based Toy Manufacturer Sued for Discrimination and Retaliation
item.description: Step2 Company, LLC, a toy and plastic products manufacturer with plants in Northern Ohio, violated federal law by failing to provide reasonable accommodations to a pregnant employee with disabilities and firing her, the U.S. The alleged conduct also violates Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, which prohibits discrimination on the basis of sex, including pregnancy, childbirth, or pregnancy-related medical conditions; violates PWFA and ADA protections against retaliation; and violates PWFA prohibitions against interference with employees’ rights under the statute. According to the suit, Step2 disciplined an assembly worker, who worked at the company’s Perrysville, Ohio facility, under its attendance policy when she was absent because of pregnancy-related medical conditions and took approved leave for chronic mental health conditions.

item.title: Leadership Change at Vantage: Former Arch Execs Grandisson, Gansberg to be Chair, CEO
item.description: Effective immediately, Marc Grandisson, the former chief executive officer of Arch Capital Group, Ltd., will become executive chairman of Vantage. Grandisson will work alongside Hendrick and the Vantage leadership team during this period. Just over a month after Howard Hughes Holdings (HHH) completed its acquisition of Vantage Group Holdings , HHH has announced a leadership transition.

item.title: ‘Super’ El Nino threatens to leave under-insured Asia behind the curve: Munich Re’s Hitesh Kotak
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Federal Appeals Court Rules New Jersey’s Assault Weapons Ban Is Unconstitutional
item.description: A federal appeals court ruled Friday that New Jersey’s bans on assault firearms and magazines that can hold more than 10 rounds are unconstitutional. Friday’s appeals court ruling in the New Jersey case goes further than a July 2024 ruling from a federal judge, who said that the state’s ban on AR-15s specifically was unconstitutional but upheld the provision barring larger magazines. This is the first time a federal appeals court has struck down a state ban on such weapons, and it comes as the U.S.

item.title: Marsh Thailand promotes Warakan Suwannaard to country sales leader
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Hippo+ expands to Virginia
item.description: Hippo has expanded Hippo+ to Virginia, following the offering’s initial launch in Central Texas last month. Hippo+ combines homeowners insurance, home warranty protection, proactive home care services, and access to dedicated advisors in a single package. Customers can choose from three coverage tiers: Basic, Core, and Enhanced.

item.title: Blue Marble partners with MAPFRE on parametric climate insurance
item.description: Blue Marble Microinsurance is working with Mapfre to expand access to parametric insurance in markets with significant protection gaps. The partnership combines Blue Marble’s product design and modeling capabilities with MAPFRE’s local market knowledge and distribution network. Blue Marble said the collaboration is intended to make climate protection more accessible while strengthening the financial resilience of vulnerable communities and businesses.

item.title: Gallagher Re hires Sisouda Dayre as operations director for APAC
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Greyling is now EPIC Construction & Design
item.description: EPIC Insurance Brokers & Consultants has rebranded Greyling Insurance Brokerage & Risk Consulting as EPIC Construction & Design, aligning the business under a unified national construction and design platform after more than a decade as part of EPIC.

item.title: Bamboo partners with MS Transverse
item.description: MGA Bamboo Insurance announced the launch of a new partnership with MS Transverse, expanding its admitted homeowners and dwelling fire capacity in California. MS Transverse enhances our ability to continue writing in challenging markets while offering more competitive and configurable pricing options for agents and their clients.” – John Chu, CEO of Bamboo Insurance. The partnership adds approximately $150 million in new admitted capacity and introduces additional options within Bamboo’s existing portfolio, increasing affordability and flexibility in high-demand, catastrophe-exposed regions.

item.title: Iran Renews Attacks on Gulf States After Another Night of US strikes
item.description: Iran launched more attacks on Washington’s Gulf allies on Saturday after a seventh straight night of U.S. strikes targeting Iranian military sites including logistics facilities, escalating the war one week after a ceasefire fell apart. Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said they struck a U.S.

item.title: $470 million advisory team joins Ameriprise from Wells Fargo
item.description: Sher Jeshiva Group has joined the branch channel of Ameriprise Financial from Wells Fargo Clearing Services with $470 million in client assets. The team cited Ameriprise’s technology, financial and estate planning capabilities, and advisor support as reasons for the move. According to Jeshiva, more than 90% of the practice’s clients have committed to transitioning to Ameriprise.

item.title: Allianz calls AI the ‘baseline’ of its business
item.description: Allianz is positioning artificial intelligence as a core part of its customer operations, with CEO Oliver Bäte describing the technology as the “baseline” of the business rather than an add-on. Allianz Board member Sirma Boshnakova said AI could allow the company to assist customers “on Sunday evenings, even when no employees are working.” Allianz has also developed an internal chatbot called Mia, which helps employees respond to simple customer questions more quickly. Allianz said its approach to AI will emphasize transparency, human oversight, fairness and data protection.

item.title: Kingstone partners with vipHomeLink and Whisker Labs
item.description: Beginning in July, eligible policyholders will receive Whisker Labs’ Ting smart sensor and three years of monitoring service at no cost. The program will be delivered through Kingstone’s new partnership with home management platform vipHomeLink . When the technology detects a hazard, Whisker Labs’ Fire Safety Team works with the homeowner to address it.

item.title: Earth is losing its ‘albedo’
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: The Week’s 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: No Summer Doldrums As Dollars Still Flow To AI
item.description: This is a weekly feature that runs down the week’s top 10 announced funding rounds in the U.S. Check out last week’s biggest funding deal roundup here . It was not a holiday week on the funding front, as a raft of largely AI-focused companies closed big rounds.

item.title: Stewart acquires majority interest in Rattikin Title
item.description: Stewart Title has acquired a majority interest in Fort Worth-based Rattikin Title Company , expanding its presence in North Texas. Founded more than 80 years ago, Rattikin provides commercial and residential title and escrow services across Texas. Stewart said the deal will support Rattikin’s expansion in Fort Worth and the broader North Texas region.
